version_constraint_observations.resolution_rule: Allows any 4.x.y where x >= 17 (minor + patch bumps). Picks highest satisfying version.
version_constraint_observations.failure_mode: Parser rejects or normalises to exact version — reported as lodash@4.17.0.

version_constraint_observations.resolution_rule: Allows 3.0.x only (patch bumps). Tilde freezes minor at 0.
version_constraint_observations.failure_mode: Tilde treated as caret — resolves to 3.0.1 anyway here, but would differ with wider gap. Or parser falls back to exact 3.0.0.

version_constraint_observations.resolution_rule: No operator means exact pin. Resolved version MUST equal declared version.
version_constraint_observations.failure_mode: Any version other than 1.3.0 reported — parser upgrade-resolved an exact pin.

version_constraint_observations.resolution_rule: Wildcard resolves to the latest dist-tag at install time. Non-deterministic across installs but lockfile pins it.
version_constraint_observations.failure_mode: Parser treats * as unresolvable → dep dropped. Or resolved to a wrong version because parser used a stale dist-tag.

version_constraint_observations.resolution_rule: Both bounds must be satisfied simultaneously. Picks highest version in the intersection.
version_constraint_observations.failure_mode: Parser rejects space-separated compound range as malformed → dep dropped. Or misreads second bound, resolves to 3.x.

version_constraint_observations.resolution_rule: OR of two sub-ranges; Bun picks highest satisfying. No stable ms@3.x existed at generation time, so ^2.0.0 wins → 2.1.3.
version_constraint_observations.failure_mode: Parser sees '||' as invalid separator → dep dropped. Or parser picks first token only (^2.0.0) without evaluating the right-hand side.

version_constraint_observations.resolution_rule: Caret with a pre-release lower bound. Semver allows stable releases in the same major to satisfy this range. Picks latest stable 4.x.
version_constraint_observations.failure_mode: Parser pins to 4.0.0-beta.1 exactly (treats pre-release caret as pinning to that pre-release). Or parser rejects the pre-release tag in the constraint entirely → dep dropped.

warnings: Pure semver-syntax stress test — failures here are parser bugs, not ecosystem drift. Each operator exercises a distinct code path in Bun's semver resolver.
C1 (caret): allows minor+patch bumps above the declared floor. Most common operator; baseline sanity for any resolution failure investigation.
C2 (tilde): allows patch bumps only. A parser that confuses tilde with caret will resolve the same version here (3.0.1) but would diverge on packages with many minor versions.
C3 (exact): lockfile-pinning pattern. Mend must report the version exactly as declared in manifest — no upgrade logic applies.

C4 (wildcard *): resolves to latest dist-tag at install time. Non-deterministic before install; deterministic after lockfile is written. If Mend re-resolves instead of reading the lockfile, it may produce a different version than what is encoded here.

C5 (compound AND): space-separated bounds. Frequently mishandled by parsers that split on whitespace without recognising the bound context. If the dep is missing from Mend output, the parser dropped the compound form.

C6 (compound OR): '||'-separated ranges. Rarely handled correctly by non-npm parsers. If ms is missing, the parser rejected the '||' token. If ms is present but at the wrong version, the parser applied only the first sub-range.

C7 (pre-release lower bound): the most failure-prone class. ^4.0.0-beta.1 is valid semver; stable 4.x releases satisfy it. A parser that refuses pre-release strings in constraints will drop zod entirely. A parser that pins to the pre-release will report zod@4.0.0-beta.1 instead of zod@4.4.3.

Bun is NOT in the Mend install-tool list. Detection is lockfile-driven only (bun.lock JSONC parsing). The version_constraint_observations above document what Mend SHOULD emit; any deviation indicates a specific semver parser bug.

Dual is-number instances: is-number@6.0.0 (via is-odd@3.0.1) and is-number@3.0.0 (via is-odd@0.1.2 → is-even@1.0.0). If Mend deduplicates to a single version, the transitive chain is not correctly traversed.
Dual is-odd instances: is-odd@3.0.1 (direct, C2 tilde) and is-odd@0.1.2 (transitive via is-even). Both must appear with distinct versions. If Mend reports only one, the tree is incomplete.
